skills.skill: Two-dimensional shapes - generalize the properties of orientation and congruence of rotations, reflections, translations, and dilations of two-dimensional shapes on a coordinate plane
skills.prompt: This topic covers Two-dimensional shapes - generalize the properties of orientation and congruence of rotations, reflections, translations, and dilations of two-dimensional shapes on a coordinate plane for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.10(A)). This aligns with TEKS standard 8.10(A). Students at this level should develop proficiency in this skill, building on prior knowledge and connecting to grade-level expectations. Key concepts include: understanding the underlying mathematical principles, applying appropriate procedures and strategies, solving both computational and contextual problems, and justifying solutions using mathematical reasoning. Students should represent the concept using multiple representations (numerical, graphical, verbal, algebraic) and make connections to real-world applications. Vocabulary and notation specific to this topic should be used precisely. Common misconceptions should be addressed through careful instruction and varied practice. Problems and explanations should be appropriate for 13-14 year olds at the Grade 8 level, progressing from concrete to abstract understanding.
